records.ety: [OE. bord, AS. bord board, shipboard; akin to bred plank, Icel. borð board, side of a ship, Goth. fōtu-baurd footstool, D. bord board, G. brett, bort. See def. 8. √92.]

defs: A piece of timber sawed thin, and of considerable length and breadth as compared with the thickness, -- used for building, etc.
notes: ☞ When sawed thick, as over one and a half or two inches, it is usually called a plank.

defs: A table to put food upon.
quotes: Fruit of all kinds . . . She gathers, tribute large, and on the board Heaps with unsparing hand.
notes: ☞ The term board answers to the modern table, but it was often movable, and placed on trestles.

defs: Hence: What is served on a table as food; stated meals; provision; entertainment; -- usually as furnished for pay; as, to work for one's board; the price of board.
A table at which a council or court is held.

A council, convened for business, or any authorized assembly or meeting, public or private; a number of persons appointed or elected to sit in council for the management or direction of some public or private business or trust; as, the Board of Admiralty; a board of trade; a board of directors, trustees, commissioners, etc.
quotes: Both better acquainted with affairs than any other who sat then at that board.
We may judge from their letters to the board.

defs: A square or oblong piece of thin wood or other material used for some special purpose, as, a molding board; a board or surface painted or arranged for a game; as, a chessboard; a backgammon board.
Paper made thick and stiff like a board, for book covers, etc.; pasteboard; as, to bind a book in boards.
The stage in a theater; as, to go upon the boards, to enter upon the theatrical profession.
The border or side of anything.
The stretch which a ship makes in one tack.
notes: ☞ Board is much used adjectively or as the last part of a compound; as, fir board, clapboard, floor board, shipboard, sideboard, ironing board, chessboard, cardboard, pasteboard, seaboard; board measure.

records.ety: [Cf. Board to accost, and see Board, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Boarded; p. pr. & vb. n. Boarding.]

defs: To cover with boards or boarding; as, to board a house.
To go on board of, or enter, as a ship, whether in a hostile or a friendly way.
quotes: You board an enemy to capture her, and a stranger to receive news or make a communication.

defs: To enter, as a railway car.
To furnish with regular meals, or with meals and lodgings, for compensation; to supply with daily meals.
To place at board, for compensation; as, to board one's horse at a livery stable.
To obtain meals, or meals and lodgings, statedly for compensation; as, he boards at the hotel.
quotes: We are several of us, gentlemen and ladies, who board in the same house.

defs: To approach; to accost; to address; hence, to woo.
quotes: I will board her, though she chide as loud As thunder when the clouds in autumn crack.
